descUpdates.shortDescription: YieldBlox is a DAO-managed money-market protocol on Stellar, built on the Blend Protocol with Soroban governance for lending and borrowing digital assets. In February 2026 one of its Blend lending pools was drained of roughly $10.8M via oracle manipulation: an attacker inflated an illiquid USTRY market on the Stellar DEX (the Reflector oracle source) to borrow out the pool's reserves. Root cause was a pool-operator oracle misconfiguration, not a Blend core flaw; ~$7.2M was frozen by Stellar validators.

Templar is a decentralized 'cypher lending' protocol that lets Stellar users borrow USDC against XLM collateral directly from their Stellar wallets, without bridges or wrapped tokens. It uses NEAR's multi-party-computation (MPC) network and Chain Signatures to custody deposits and settle cross-chain. On Stellar the collateral asset is XLM (not Bitcoin); USDC borrowing is also available on Ethereum, Solana, NEAR and other chains. The Stellar integration was announced November 2025.

ZKLiquid has rebranded to LiquidsFi (liquids.fi). It is a decentralized cross-chain liquidity protocol and interoperability/oracle network that aims to eliminate liquidity fragmentation by securely transmitting on-chain data and liquidity across blockchains. The LiquidsFi Interoperability Protocol (LIP) targets connectivity between EVM and non-EVM chains, making it one of the first protocols enabling production-grade interoperability between Stellar's Soroban and EVM chains.

Steepx is a ledger explorer for the Stellar network that provides detailed insights into operations, transactions, assets, and exchanges. It supports multiple networks and offers features like transaction history and real-time asset data to help users navigate the Stellar ecosystem. Steepx received Stellar Community Fund support across SCF rounds 16 and 20, totaling $31,100.

TrustSwap (a.k.a. Team Finance) is a DeFi token-management platform offering token minting, token and liquidity locks, NFT locks, multisender, staking and vesting-as-a-service. It received a Stellar Community Fund award (SCF #36, Financial Protocols track) to bring its token-management and locking tooling to Stellar/Soroban. Note: TrustSwap originated on EVM chains, and no live Soroban mainnet deployment is independently confirmed beyond the SCF award; its earlier listing was generic Team Finance copy with no Stellar specifics.

Bridge is a stablecoin infrastructure company (co-founded 2022 by Zach Abrams and Sean Yu). Stripe's ~$1.1B acquisition of Bridge — its largest ever — was announced October 2024 and CLOSED on February 4, 2025 (not 2024). On Stellar, Bridge issues MGUSD, MoneyGram's U.S.-dollar-backed stablecoin native to the Stellar blockchain, with tokens minted/burned via M0's smart-contract infrastructure.

Allbridge enables users to access the Stellar ecosystem through cross-chain bridge swaps. Via Allbridge Core, Stellar joined as a supported chain (using Soroban) to offer native USDC and stablecoin cross-chain swaps without token wrapping, connecting Stellar to chains like Ethereum, Solana, Polygon, Base, Arbitrum and OP Mainnet — letting Stellar protocols tap cross-chain stablecoin liquidity.

African fiat-to-stablecoin anchor (HoneyCoin) headquartered in Nairobi, Kenya, settling USDC/USDT on/off-ramps across 18+ markets (Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, Tanzania, Uganda, etc.) via mobile money and bank rails, including USDC cash-out at MoneyGram on Stellar.

BSP-regulated Philippine anchor and exchange providing PHP fiat on/off-ramps and remittances on Stellar, with 33,000+ cash-in/cash-out locations; an early Stellar PHP anchor and issuer of the peso-backed PHPC stablecoin.
LATAM cross-border payments anchor (cashabroad.one) enabling businesses to send and receive instant, low-cost international payments via USDC on Stellar.
Brazil-focused, Stellar-backed anchor offering BRL stablecoin on/off-ramps and FX liquidity for cross-border payments, processing deposits and withdrawals via PIX with full compliance.

Argentine-founded neo-bank (letsping.com) for Latin American freelancers and contractors, providing US-dollar accounts, crypto accounts and invoicing so users can receive and convert payments in USD or crypto across borders.

East African Stellar anchor (ClickPesa) based in Tanzania enabling cross-border B2B payments that settle into Tanzanian Shilling (TZS) on local bank and mobile-money rails, connecting senders abroad to recipients in Tanzania.

IDT's BOSS Money (formerly Leaf Global Fintech), a Stellar-based remittance and digital-wallet service for migrants and refugees across East and Francophone Africa (Rwanda, Uganda, Kenya, DRC, Senegal, Cameroon, Côte d'Ivoire), letting users store and send cross-border value.

Latin American anchor-as-a-service (alfredpay.io) providing USDC on/off-ramps on Stellar, with remittance corridors from the US into the Dominican Republic and Haiti and crypto-to-local-fiat payment facilitation for fintechs across LATAM.

newRecords.name: Rabet
newRecords.shortDescription: Open-source non-custodial wallet built natively for the Stellar network, available as a browser extension for Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Brave. Lets users manage XLM and Stellar assets, transfer and exchange assets, and sign/connect to Stellar dApps (including Soroban apps) by injecting Rabet into the browser without an SDK. Source: rabet.io, github.com/rabetofficial/rabet-extension.

newRecords.name: QuickNode
newRecords.shortDescription: Managed RPC infrastructure provider with official Stellar support. Offers Stellar Mainnet and Testnet endpoints exposing the Horizon REST API for ledger/account/transaction queries plus the Soroban JSON-RPC API for smart-contract deployment and invocation, removing the need to self-host nodes. Source: QuickNode docs and 'QuickNode Announces Support for Stellar and Its Horizon API' blog.

newRecords.name: Validation Cloud
newRecords.shortDescription: Node infrastructure provider offering high-performance Stellar RPC and Horizon nodes via its Node API. Live on Soroban (Stellar's smart-contract platform) mainnet since March 2024, combining Horizon and Stellar/Soroban RPC in a single endpoint with multi-region, failover, and free-tier access for building and querying Stellar dApps. Sources: validationcloud.io/stellar, blog.validationcloud.io/stellar.

newRecords.name: Squid Router
newRecords.shortDescription: Cross-chain swap and bridge aggregator (built on Axelar) that went live for Stellar at the Axelar-Stellar integration launch on Feb 16, 2026. Confirmed live and operational at launch, it routes liquidity and bridges assets such as XLM into/out of Stellar across connected chains. Source: Axelar Network integrates Stellar announcement (Feb 2026).
